free_text: Results of a low-background measurement of the fission
neutron spectrum from Cf-252 in the 9- to 29-MeV
energy range
Experiment performed in a mine in Bad Blei-
Berg, Carinthia, Austria, to minimize cosmic radi-
ation. The effective shielding was more than 1000 m
of rock.
R.Boettger
Experimental details
The fission neutron energies were determined by
time-of-flight techniques, using a flight path of
279.6+/-0.2 cm. The contribution from wall scattered
neutrons was determined by inserting a cone-shaped
water-filled shadow bar between the fission chamber
and the neutron detector.
N-gamma pulse-shape discrimination was applied.
The 252Cf source was enclosed in a fast fission chamber
A calibrated NE-213 liquid scintillation detec-
tor, 25.4 cm in diameter and 5.08 cm thick, was used
to detect the fission neutrons. This detector gave the
TOF start signal. The efficiency of the neutron detec-
tor up to 18-20 MeV was determined with the MONTE
CARLO code NEFF4. For higher energies the efficiency
was determined from hydrogen cross section data. Four
different biases were used, namely at 2.0, 2.75, 4.0
and 5.0 MeV.
A fast fission chamber, containing the Cf-252
source (1.5643e+5 fissions/sec +/-0.2 %, was used to
detect the fission fragments and giving the TOF stop
signal. The fission chamber efficiency was
Fission neutrons.
Fission fragments.
.The data were corrected for correlated and random
background.
.Different selections from the same experimental data
represented in SUBENTs 22201-003 - 22202-007 are
correlated with each other. One may combine results
from different bias settings and different energy
groups to generate a new continuous set. Though no
combination of data from different bias settings
except of overlapping or identical energy intervals
is allowed
Total error, including statistical and systema-
tic errors, the latter dominated by the neutron detec-
tor efficiency. ERR-2 and ERR-8 not included. They
are negligible.
Fission chamber efficiency error
Neutron detector solid angle error
Neutron-fission registration error
Cf-252 half life error. Calculated by the
last compiler as 1% of 2.62 YR
.Data taken from main ref.
N.O.

codes corrected in SAN 003, 004. Data in SAN 003
split in four SUBENTS: 003, 005, 006, 007.
SD: INC-SOURCE -> SAMPLE.
